collections.name: AI/HPC
collections.summary: Skills for launching, validating, and troubleshooting distributed AI workloads on Slurm-backed HPC systems.

collections.name: Bioinformatics Workflows
collections.summary: Domain skills for running reviewed nf-core, GATK, and BLAST bioinformatics workflows on Slurm-backed HPC systems.

collections.name: Containers
collections.summary: Skills for building, validating, running MPI containers, and staging data for containerized HPC workloads.

collections.name: Core HPC
collections.summary: Starter skills for Slurm jobs, arrays, dependency chains, pending reason triage, efficiency review, restartable workflows, notebooks, IDE tunnels, OpenMP placement, debugging, and storage triage.

collections.name: Data Movement
collections.summary: Skills for staging, transferring, validating, and managing research data on HPC storage.

collections.name: Facility Operations
collections.summary: Read-only operational skills for usage reporting, pending reason triage, node triage, and module tree health.

collections.name: GPU, MPI, And Performance
collections.summary: Skills for validating GPU allocations, GPU memory failures, Ray, NCCL communication, DeepSpeed and PyTorch DDP launches, MPI, containerized MPI, and mpi4py launches, OpenMP placement, Slurm efficiency review, and first-pass performance evidence.

collections.name: Scheduler Basics
collections.summary: Starter skills for submitting and comparing basic jobs across Slurm, PBS-style, LSF, HTCondor, and Grid Engine schedulers.

collections.name: Simulation Workflows
collections.summary: Domain skills for MPI/GPU-heavy simulation, electronic-structure, CFD, and weather workloads on Slurm-backed HPC systems.

collections.name: Software Stacks
collections.summary: Skills for debugging modules, checking module tree health, compiler/MPI compatibility, Python, Ray, Dask, mpi4py, R, Julia, MATLAB, Conda environments, IDE tunnels, containers, containerized MPI, and reproducible HPC software stacks.

collections.name: Training And Onboarding
collections.summary: Skills for teaching new HPC users, including Slurm jobs, notebooks, IDE tunnels, Python, R, Julia, and MATLAB workloads, and workshop environments.

collections.name: Workflow Engines
collections.summary: Skills for launching portable workflow engines, CWL/WDL runs, Dask worker clusters, and lightweight Slurm dependency chains.

site_adapters.name: Example Campus Cluster
site_adapters.summary: A non-production adapter showing how a site can map generic skills to local HPC policy.

skills.description: This skill provides conservative Slurm templates for launching MPI applications in Apptainer or Singularity containers, including hybrid MPI and host-MPI bind model checks.

examples.title: Launch a hybrid MPI container job with Slurm
Launch a bind-model MPI container job with host MPI
Inspect host and container MPI assumptions

skills.name: Apptainer MPI On Slurm
skills.summary: Run MPI applications from Apptainer containers inside Slurm allocations.

skills.description: This skill provides templates for executing Apptainer or Singularity images under Slurm with explicit bind mounts, working directories, and optional GPU support.

examples.title: Run a container job under Slurm

skills.name: Apptainer Run Container
skills.summary: Run Apptainer containers safely on shared HPC systems.

skills.description: This skill provides a conservative Slurm pattern for local BLAST+ searches, including a tiny FASTA smoke test, local database creation with makeblastdb, explicit CPU and output bounds, and dry-run-first launch records.

examples.title: Plan or run a local BLAST+ smoke test on Slurm

skills.name: BLAST On Slurm

references.title: BLAST Command Line Applications User Manual

skills.summary: Run local BLAST+ searches on Slurm with bounded smoke-test defaults.

skills.description: This skill helps users wrap long-running work with checkpoint directories, restart detection, progress state, and Slurm signal handling patterns that make reruns safer and less wasteful.
skills.name: Checkpoint Restart Workflow
skills.summary: Structure long HPC jobs so they can resume after time limits or preemption.

skills.description: This skill helps users create read-only checksum manifests for selected input or output files before transfer, archival, or support handoff.
skills.name: Checksum Manifest Create
skills.summary: Create checksum manifests for transfer validation and reproducibility.

skills.description: This skill helps HPC support teams collect portable, read-only Slurm accounting, queue, and partition evidence for usage reviews without changing scheduler or cluster state.

examples.title: Collect a read-only Slurm usage report

skills.name: Cluster Usage Report Readonly
skills.summary: Collect read-only Slurm usage evidence for facility support reports.

skills.description: This skill gathers read-only evidence about loaded modules, compiler paths, MPI wrapper commands, and optional MPI smoke compilation so users can avoid mismatched compiler and MPI stacks.

examples.title: Collect compiler and MPI wrapper report

skills.name: Compiler MPI Matrix
skills.summary: Check compiler, MPI wrapper, and module compatibility before building HPC codes.

skills.description: This skill helps users create Conda/Mamba environments in explicit prefixes, manage package caches, export environment metadata, and avoid common HPC filesystem and module conflicts.
skills.name: Conda Mamba On HPC
skills.summary: Create Conda or Mamba environments while protecting shared HPC filesystems.

skills.description: This skill helps users prepare reproducible container definitions, choose site-approved build locations, record build metadata, and avoid expensive or policy-sensitive image builds on shared login filesystems.

examples.title: Record or run a container build plan

skills.name: Container Build For HPC
skills.summary: Plan and build Apptainer-compatible images for shared HPC systems.

skills.description: This skill provides a conservative Slurm pattern for CP2K calculations, including MPI/OpenMP layout, CP2K data file checks, generated input from a template, output and restart record keeping, and dry-run-first smoke-test controls.

examples.title: Plan or run a CP2K smoke test on Slurm

skills.name: CP2K On Slurm
skills.summary: Run CP2K calculations on Slurm with MPI/OpenMP layout and restart planning.

skills.description: This skill provides a conservative pattern for running Common Workflow Language workflows with cwltool inside a Slurm allocation, including output, cache, and temporary directory controls, a tiny CommandLineTool smoke test, and dry-run-first launch records.

skills.summary: Run small CWL workflows inside Slurm allocations with cwltool.

skills.summary: Launch Dask workers through Slurm with dask-jobqueue and bounded dry-run defaults.

skills.name: Dataset Staging To Scratch
skills.summary: Stage inputs to scratch, run work, and collect outputs from Slurm jobs.

skills.description: This skill provides a conservative Slurm wrapper for DeepSpeed jobs, including environment capture, storage and checkpoint reminders, a reviewable launch plan, and an optional tiny DeepSpeed smoke test.

skills.name: DeepSpeed On Slurm
skills.summary: Plan and smoke test DeepSpeed launches on Slurm GPU allocations.

skills.description: This skill provides a cautious EasyBuild workflow for searching easyconfigs, dry-running dependency resolution, and installing software into a user-controlled prefix.
skills.name: EasyBuild Install Software
skills.summary: Install scientific software with EasyBuild and generated modules.
